Title: Safeguarding Traditional Palestinian Food Culture
Object Description (Abstract): Examines Palestinian food culture as 'culinary sumud' and resistance to cultural erasure through analysis of Food and Fadwa play and UNESCO heritage frameworks.
Disciplines: Food Studies; Cultural Heritage; Literary Studies

Title: We Palestinian Refugees
Object Description (Abstract): Explores heritage practices in refugee camps using ethnography, focusing on thobe as cultural identity and resistance.

Title: Representations of Palestinian Culture in the Digital Public Sphere
Object Description (Abstract): Analyzes social media representations of cultural symbols like keffiyeh and thobe as digital resistance narratives.

Title: Fragmented Space and Violence in Palestine
Object Description (Abstract): Examines how spatial fragmentation reshapes political mobility and increases violence using qualitative analysis of Oslo-era geography.

Title: Forced or Development Induced Displacement
Object Description (Abstract): Argues displacement is produced through annexation policies and urban planning strategies in occupied territories.

Title: The Palestinian Nakba and its Continuous Repercussions
Object Description (Abstract): Frames the Nakba as an ongoing structural condition shaping identity, displacement, and long-term consequences.

Title: Struggling for Time: Environmental Governance and Agrarian Resistance
Object Description (Abstract): Examines environmental governance as a mechanism of control and resistance through ethnographic fieldwork in agrarian contexts.

Title: Dwelling on the Green Line
Object Description (Abstract): Analyzes settlement expansion and housing privatization as mechanisms of territorial control and annexation.

Title: Static Displacement, Adaptive Domesticity
Object Description (Abstract): Explores how communities adapt to displacement through spatial practices under militarized planning regimes.

Title: Geographies of the Body: Music, Violence and Manhood in Palestine
Object Description (Abstract): A community-based qualitative study examining how music practices intersect with masculinity, violence, and identity formation in Palestinian society, showing how sound and performance are embedded in political and social life.

Title: Intermediality and Cultural Journalism
Object Description (Abstract): Analyzes cultural journalism and artistic production in response to the 1967 Naksa, exploring how media, literature, and performative cultural formsâ€”including musical expressionâ€”shape narratives of trauma, memory, and political identity.

Title: Tweet Like Itâ€™s Free: Civic Imagination in the 2021 Palestinian Unity Intifada
Object Description (Abstract): Examines digital expression during the Unity Intifada, where Palestinians used creative and rhythmic forms of online communication to imagine liberation, functioning similarly to performative and collective cultural expression seen in music and protest.
Keywords/Subjects/Tags: digital media; activism; civic imagination; cultural expression

Title: Authoritarianism in the Digital Age
Creator/Author: Jarrett, K.; Kitchin, R.; Hiah, J.; Knight Steele
Object Description (Abstract): This text discusses the evolving nature of authoritarianism in the digital age, specifically how governments use technology to control populations, consolidate power, and erode democracy,Ê and discusses forms of resistance to this authoritarianism. As an introduction to a journal edition, it also references relevant articles on more specific topics.

Title: How Surveillance Tech is Used to Oppress Palestinians Through Apartheid?
Object Description (Abstract): This video describes the effects of constant surveillance on the population in Palestine and briefly what Palestinians are doing, and what other people can do, to combat this.

Title: Settler Colonialism, Surveillance, and Fear
Object Description (Abstract): This text, a chapter from a book, frames surveillance in Israel/Palestine through a settler-colonial lens, emphasizing the role of fear in (geopolitical, biopolitical, and necropolitical) control.
